name: Crepes with Fresh Berry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Crepes: (Makes about twelve 6- to 7-inch crepes)
Beat the eggs in a large mixing bowl. In a separate bowl, combine the flour, salt, and sugar. In another bowl, mix together the sherry and milk. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the eggs, alternating with the milk mixture. Beat until smooth. Cover and let the batter rest for 30 minutes.
Heat a crepe pan over moderate heat and lightly brush with melted butter. When the butter is hot, pour in enough batter to cover the bottom of the pan, tilting the pan to spread the batter evenly. Cook until brown underneath, then quickly turn and cook the other side. Continue cooking crepes, adding more butter as needed, until all the batter is used.
Sauce: Reserve 1/4 cup berries for garnish. In a bowl, combine the berries with sugar to taste. Add the sherry and mix lightly.
To serve, fill each crepe with a small scoop of vanilla ice cream and 2 tablespoons of the sauce. Fold the crepe as desired and top with a small dollop of whipped cream. Garnish with fresh berries. Serves 4 to 6.
